name: Do I need to replace the threshold when installing a new door?

mainEntity.name: When should the threshold be replaced instead of reused?
mainEntity.text: When should the threshold be replaced instead of reused?

acceptedAnswer.text: The threshold is one of the most important parts of a door system because it helps block water, air, and pests. If it is cracked, soft, warped, uneven, or already leaking, it is usually smarter to replace it during the door installation rather than trying to reuse it. A bad threshold can cause drafts, water intrusion, and latch problems even when the new door itself is high quality. In coastal areas, threshold condition matters even more because wind-driven rain and moisture can reach the entry quickly. If the threshold is still structurally sound and the fit is correct, it may be reusable, but the installer should inspect it carefully before making that call. Ask whether threshold replacement is included in the quote or treated as an extra item, because this detail often affects both cost and long-term performance.
acceptedAnswer.description: A threshold should usually be replaced if it is cracked, rotted, warped, leaking, or too worn to seal properly. Reusing a damaged threshold can undermine a new door even if the slab is brand new. If the existing threshold is solid and properly aligned, it may be reusable.
